question:
What is the primary reason "social loafing" — exerting less effort in a group than alone — is considered a structural problem rather than purely a character flaw?
A) Group members are biologically weaker than individuals.
B) Reduced individual accountability in groups creates conditions where reduced effort is the rational response, regardless of personal work ethic.
C) Social loafing only occurs in groups larger than 100 people.
D) It is a character flaw and has no structural dimension.
